req.title: Hr-Admin updates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates UI-level update of a performance-review by an hr-admin with mandatory-field validation and audit trail. Tests are derived using: EP for valid/invalid data partitions, BVA for field length limits, STC for authenticated→submitted→confirmed state flow, DT for role/auth combinations, and NEG for forbidden role and unauthenticated access. Each test uses fixtures for browser setup and API teardown to stay idempotent.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_performance_review_success
preconditions: A performance-review record with id <REVIEW_ID> exists
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Verify audit record is written via API
expected: Form submission returns HTTP 200 or 201
Updated entity id is present in response
Audit record exists for the update action
UI shows success notification
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_updates_performance_review_success(hr_admin_page, existing_review, audit_api_client):
    review_id = existing_review["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")

    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "rating": "4",
        "comments": "Good performance",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1"
    })

    page.click("[data-testid='submit-review']")

    success_msg = page.locator("[data-testid='success-notification']")
    success_msg.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    assert success_msg.is_visible(), "Success notification not shown"

    resp = audit_api_client.get_latest_audit(entity_id=review_id, action="update")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    audit = resp.json()
    assert audit["entity_id"] == str(review_id)
    assert audit["action"] == "update"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "hr-admin"

tests.name: test_update_performance_review_missing_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error
preconditions: A performance-review record with id <REVIEW_ID> exists
actions: Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Form submission is blocked or returns HTTP 400
Validation error message is displayed for the missing field
No audit record is written
Review record is not changed in the backend
trace: R-WEB-022: audit record only on success
tests.executable:
def test_update_performance_review_missing_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error(
    hr_admin_page, existing_review, audit_api_client
):
    review_id = existing_review["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")

    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "rating": "",
        "comments": "Some comment",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1"
    })

    page.click("[data-testid='submit-review']")

    error_msg = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-rating']")
    assert error_msg.is_visible(), "Validation error for 'rating' not displayed"

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url) as resp_info:
        pass
    if resp_info.value:
        assert resp_info.value.status == 400

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get_latest_audit(entity_id=review_id, action="update")
    assert audit_resp.status_code != 200 or audit_resp.json().get("count", 0) == 0, \
        "Audit record must not exist after failed validation"

tests.name: test_update_performance_review_comments_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: A performance-review record with id <REVIEW_ID> exists
Maximum allowed length for 'comments' field is 500 characters
actions: Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
Then repeat with 501 characters and assert rejection
expected: 500-character comments: form submits successfully (200/201)
501-character comments: form is rejected with validation error (400)
Audit record written only for the 500-character submission
tests.executable:
def test_update_performance_review_comments_at_max_boundary_length(
    hr_admin_page, existing_review, audit_api_client
):
    review_id = existing_review["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    MAX_LEN = 500

    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")
    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "rating": "3",
        "comments": "A" * MAX_LEN,
        "review_period": "2024-Q2"
    })
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-review']")
    success = page.locator("[data-testid='success-notification']")
    success.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    assert success.is_visible(), "Expected success at max boundary (500 chars)"

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get_latest_audit(entity_id=review_id, action="update")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200

    <reset_review_to_original>(existing_review)

    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")
    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "rating": "3",
        "comments": "A" * (MAX_LEN + 1),
        "review_period": "2024-Q2"
    })
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-review']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-comments']")
    assert error.is_visible(), "Expected validation error above max boundary (501 chars)"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_performance_review
preconditions: A performance-review record with id <REVIEW_ID> exists
actions: Attempt to navigate to /performance-reviews/<REVIEW_ID>/edit without authentication
expected: User is redirected to login page OR receives HTTP 401
Review form is not accessible
No audit record is written
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_performance_review(
    browser_context_factory, existing_review
):
    review_id = existing_review["id"]
    context = browser_context_factory(authenticated=False)
    page = context.new_page()

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url or "/login" in r.url
    ) as resp_info:
        page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")

    final_url = page.url
    assert "/login" in final_url or resp_info.value.status == 401, (
        f"Expected redirect to login or 401, got url={final_url} "
        f"status={resp_info.value.status}"
    )

    form = page.locator("[data-testid='review-edit-form']")
    assert not form.is_visible(), "Edit form must not be visible to unauthenticated user"

    context.close()

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_performance_review
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A performance-review record with id <REVIEW_ID> exists
actions: Observe HTTP response or UI feedback
expected: HTTP 403 is returned OR user is shown an access-denied page
Review edit form is not rendered
No audit record is written for an update attempt
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_performance_review(
    browser_context_factory, existing_review, audit_api_client
):
    review_id = existing_review["id"]
    context = browser_context_factory(authenticated=True, role="employee")
    page = context.new_page()

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url
    ) as resp_info:
        page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")

    status = resp_info.value.status
    assert status == 403 or page.locator("[data-testid='access-denied']").is_visible(), (
        f"Expected 403 or access-denied UI, got status={status}"
    )

    form = page.locator("[data-testid='review-edit-form']")
    assert not form.is_visible(), "Edit form must not be accessible to forbidden role"

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get_latest_audit(entity_id=review_id, action="update")
    count = audit_resp.json().get("count", 0) if audit_resp.status_code == 200 else 0
    assert count == 0, "No audit record should be written for forbidden access attempt"

    context.close()

tests.name: test_state_transition_draft_to_submitted_performance_review
preconditions: A performance-review in state 'draft' exists with id <REVIEW_ID>
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Verify state change and audit record
expected: Performance-review status transitions from 'draft' to 'submitted'
Response is 200 or 201 with entity id
Audit record captures state transition with old and new state
UI reflects new 'submitted' status
trace: R-WEB-022: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
def test_state_transition_draft_to_submitted_performance_review(
    hr_admin_page, draft_review, audit_api_client
):
    review_id = draft_review["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit")

    assert page.locator("[data-testid='status-badge']").inner_text() == "draft"

    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "rating": "5",
        "comments": "Excellent quarter",
        "review_period": "2024-Q3"
    })
    page.select_option("[data-testid='status-select']", "submitted")
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-review']")

    success = page.locator("[data-testid='success-notification']")
    success.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    assert success.is_visible()

    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}")
    badge = page.locator("[data-testid='status-badge']")
    assert badge.inner_text() == "submitted", "Status badge must show 'submitted'"

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get_latest_audit(entity_id=review_id, action="update")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit = audit_resp.json()
    assert audit["previous_state"] == "draft"
    assert audit["new_state"] == "submitted"
